body.title: Deposit high-importance consolidation patterns as palace theme nodes
body.result: upsert_theme_palace() helper + _THEME_IMPORTANCE_THRESHOLD config; called after cortex.store() for importance >= 0.75
body.description:
consolidation.py extracts durable patterns (FACTUAL, INTERPRETIVE, PROCEDURAL) via LLM and deposits them to clan.memories via cortex.store(). It NEVER writes to palace (adc.palace). xMemory's 'themes' level is exactly this: high-importance patterns that have graduated beyond the episodic/semantic layer and deserve a navigable top-level node.

Amend consolidation.py: after cortex.store() succeeds for a result with importance >= 0.75, upsert a node into adc.palace at path theigors/themes/{keyword_slug} (slug from first keyword, max 40 chars). If the node already exists, append the new narrative as a bullet to its content (don't overwrite — accumulate). If it doesn't exist, create with content = narrative + '\n\nSource: consolidation pass'. Set node_type='theme', updated_at=now().
Non-blocking: if palace write fails, log and continue — clan.memories deposit already succeeded.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/consolidation.py; adc.palace table (UPSERT)
**Design rules:** non-blocking on palace write failure; importance threshold 0.75 (env IGOR_THEME_IMPORTANCE_THRESHOLD); accumulate don't overwrite for existing nodes; slug from first keyword only
**Scope boundary:** In: palace UPSERT for high-importance consolidation results, slug from keyword, accumulate content. Out: changing consolidation clustering logic, changing clan.memories deposit, reading or modifying existing palace nodes other than theigors/themes/*.
**Test plan:** consolidation cycle with cluster yielding importance=0.8 → adc.palace row at theigors/themes/{slug} created. Second consolidation cycle for same theme → content has both narratives. importance=0.6 → no palace row. palace write failure → clan.memories deposit still recorded (non-blocking verified).
